author: John Chrysostom
book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.

ccel.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

title: Matthew X. 34.

source.title: NPNF1-10. St. Chrysostom: Homilies on the Gospel of Saint Matthew

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Think not that I am cometo send peace on earth; I am not cometo send peace, but a sword.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, He sets forth the things that are more painful, and that with great aggravation: and the objection they were sure to meet Him with, He prevents them by stating.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, lest hearing this, they should say, “For, this then art Thou come, to destroy both us, and them that obey us, and to fill the earth with war?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He first saith Himself, “I am not come to send peace on earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then did He enjoin them to pronounce peace on entering into each house?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And again, how did the angels say, “Glory to God in the highest, and on earth peace”?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And how came all the prophets too to publish it for good tidings?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because this more than anything is peace, when the diseased is cut off, when the mutinous is removed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For thus it is possible for Heaven to be united to earth.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since the physician too in this way preserves the rest of the body, when he amputates the incurable part; and the general, when he has brought to a separation them that were agreed in mischief.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus it came to pass also in the case of that famous tower; for their evil peacewas ended by their good discord, and peace made thereby.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus Paul also divided them that were conspiring against him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And in Naboth’s case that agreement was at the same time more grievous than any war.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For concord is not in every case a good thing, since even robbers agree together.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: The war is not then the effect of His purpose, but of their temper.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For His will indeed was that all should agree in the word of godliness; but because they fell to dissension, war arises.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet He spake not so; but what saith He?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “I am not come to send peace;” comforting them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As if He said, For think not that ye are to blame for these things; it is I who order them so, because men are so disposed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be not ye therefore confounded, as though the events happened against expectation.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To this end am I come, to send war among men; for this is my will.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be not ye therefore troubled, when the earth is at war, as though it were subject to some hostile device.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when the worse part is rent away, then after that Heaven is knit unto the better.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things He saith, as strengthening them against the evil suspicion of the multitude.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He said not “war,” but what was more grievous than it, “a sword.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if there be somewhat painful in these expressions, and of an alarming emphasis, marvel not.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, it being His will to train their ears by the severity of His words, lest in their difficult circumstances they should start aside, He fashioned His discourse accordingly; lest any one should say it was by flattery He persuaded them, and by concealing the hardships; therefore even to those things which merited to be otherwise expressed, He gave by His words the more galling and painful turn.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For it is better to see persons’ gentleness in things, than in words.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore neither with this was He satisfied, but unfolds also the very nature of the war, signifying it to be far more grievous even than a civil war; and He saith, “I am come to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and the daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not friends only, saith He, nor fellow citizens, but even kinsmen shall stand against one another, and nature shall be divided against herself.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For I am come,” saith He, “to set a man at variance against his father, and the daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, not merely among those of the same household is the war, but among those that are dearest, and extremely near to each other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this more than anything signifies His power, that hearing these things, they both accepted Him, and set about persuading all others.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet was it not He that did this: of course not: but the wickedness of the other sort: nevertheless He saith it is His own doing.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For such is the custom of the Scripture.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, and elsewhere also He saith, “God hath given them eyes that they should not see:”and here He speaks in this way, in order that having, as I said before, exercised themselves in these words, they might not be confounded on suffering reproaches and insults.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if any think these things intolerable, let them be reminded of an ancient history.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For in times of old also this came to pass, which thing especially shows the old covenant to be akin to the new, and Him who is here speaking, the same with the giver of those commands.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean that in the case of the Jews also, when each had slain his neighbor, then He laid aside His anger against them; both when they made the calf, and when they were joined to Baal Peor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Where then are they that say, “That God is evil, and this good?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For behold He hath filled the world with blood, shed by kinsmen.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nevertheless even this we affirm to be a work of great love towards man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore, you see, implying that it was He who approved those other acts also, He makes mention also of a prophecy, which if not spoken for this end, yet involves the same meaning.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And what is this?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “A man’s foes shall be they of his own household.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed among the Jews also something of the kind took place.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: That is, there were prophets, and false prophets, and the people was divided, and families were in dissension; and some believed the one, and some the other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore the prophet admonishes, saying, “Trust ye not in friends, have not hope in guides; yea, even of her that lieth in thy bosom beware, in respect of communicating aught to her:” and, “A man’s enemies are the men that are in his own house.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He said, preparing him that should receive the word to be above all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For to die is not evil, but to die an evil death.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: On this account He said moreover, “I am come to cast fire upon the earth.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this He said, to declare the vehemence and warmth of the love which He required.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, because He loved us very much, so He will likewise be loved of us.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these sayings would strengthenthe persons present also, and lift them higher.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For if those others,” saith He, “are to despise kinsmen, and children, and parents, imagine what manner of men ye their teachers ought to be.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since neither will the hardships stop with you, but will also pass on to the rest.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For since I am come bringing great blessings, I demand also great obedience, and purpose of heart.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He that loveth father or mother more than me, is not worthy of me; and he that loveth son or daughter more than me, is not worthy of me; and he that taketh not his cross and followeth after me, is not worthy of me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou a teacher’s dignity?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou, how He signifies himself a true Son of Him that begat Him, commanding us to let go all things beneath, and to take in preference the love of Him?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “And why speak I,” saith He, “of friends and kinsmen?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even if it be thine own life which thou preferrest to my love, thy place is far from my disciples.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Are not these things contrary to the Old Testament?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Far from it, rather they are very much in harmony therewith.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there too He commands not only to hate the worshippers of idols, but even to stone them; and in Deuteronomy again, admiring these, He saith, “Who said unto his father, and to his mother, I have not seen thee; neither did he acknowledge his brethren, and his own sons he disowned: he kept Thy oracles.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if Paul gives many directions touching parents, commanding us to obey them in all things, marvel not; for in those things only doth he mean us to obey, as many as do not hinder godliness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For indeed it is a sacred duty to render them all other honors: but when they demand more than is due, one ought not to obey.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this reason Luke saith, “If any man come to me, and hate not his father, and mother, and wife, and children, and brethren, and sisters, yea, and his own life also, he cannot be my disciple;”not commanding simply to hate them, since this were even quite contrary to the law; but “when one desires to be loved more than I am, hate him in this respect.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For this ruins both the beloved himself, and the lover.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And these things He said, both to render the children more determined, and to make the fathers more gentle, that would hinder them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For when they saw He had such strength and power as to sever their children from them, they, as attempting things impossible, would even desist.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore also He leaves the fathers, and addresses His discourse to the children, instructing the former not to make the attempt, as attempting things impracticable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then lest they should be indignant, or count it hard, see which way He makes His argument tend: in that having said, “Who hateth not father and mother,” He adds, “and his own life.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For why dost thou speak to me of parents, saith He, and brothers, and sisters, and wife?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nothing is nearer than the life to any man: yet if thou hate not this also, thou must bear in all things the opposite of his lot who loveth me.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And not even simply to hate it was His command, but so as to expose it to war, and to battles, and to slaughters, and blood.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For he that beareth not his cross, and cometh after me, cannot be my disciple.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus He said not merely that we must stand against death, but also against a violent death; and not violent only, but ignominious too.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And He discourses nothing as yet of His own passion, that when they had been for a time instructed in these things, they might more easily receive His word concerning it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Is there not, therefore, cause for amazement, how on their hearing these things, their soul did not wing its way from the body, the hardships being everywhere at hand, and the good things in expectation?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How then did it not flee away?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Great was both the power of the speaker, and the love of the hearers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wherefore though hearing things far more intolera ble and galling than those great men, Moses and Jeremiah, they continued to obey, and to say nothing against it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He that findeth his life,” saith He, “shall lose it: and he that loseth his life for my sake, shall find it.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou how great the damage to such as love it unduly? how great the gain to them that hate it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I mean, because the injunctions were disagreeable, when He was bidding them set themselves against parents, and children, and nature, and kindred, and the world, and their very soul, He sets forth the profit also, being very great.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus, “These things,” saith He, “so far from harming, will very greatly profit; and their opposites will injure;” urging them, as He ever doth, by the very things which they desire.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For why art thou willing to despise thy life?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because thou lovest it?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then for that very reason despise it, and so thou wilt advantage it in the highest degree, and do the part of one that loves it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And mark an instance of unspeakable consideration.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not in respect of our parents only doth He practise this reasoning, nor of our children, but with regard to our life, which is nearer than all; that the other point may thenceforth become unquestionable, and they may learn that they will in this way profit those of their kindred likewise, as much as may be; since so it is in the case even of our life, which is more essential to us than all.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now these things were enough to recommend men to receive them, their appointed healers.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, who would choose but receive with all readiness them that were so noble, such true heroes, and as lions running about the earth, and despising all that pertained to themselves, so that others might be saved?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet nevertheless He proffers also another reward, indicating that He is caring here for the entertainers more than for the guests.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And the first honor He confers is by saying,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “He that receiveth you, receiveth me, and he that receiveth me, receiveth Him that sent me.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: With this, what may compare? that one should receive the Father and the Son!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But He holds out herewith another reward also.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “ He,” saith He, “that receiveth a prophet in the name of a prophet, shall receive a prophet’s reward; and he that receiveth a righteous man in the name of a righteous man, shall receive a righteous man’s reward.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And as before He threatens punishment to such as do not receive them, here He defines also a certain refreshmentfor the good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to teach thee His greater care for them, He said not simply, “He that receiveth a prophet,” or “He that receiveth a righteous man,” but subjoined, “in the name of a prophet,” and, “in the name of a righteous man;” that is, if not for any worldly preferment, nor for any other temporal thing, he receive him, but because he is either a prophet or a righteous man, he shall receive a prophet’s reward, and a righteous man’s reward; such as it were meet for him to have, that hath received a prophet, or a righteous man; or, such as that other is himself to receive.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Which kind of thing Paul also said: “That your abundance may be a supply for their want, that their abundance also may be a supply for your want.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Then, lest any one should allege poverty, He saith,

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Or whosoever shall give to drink unto one of these little ones a cup of cold water only in the name of a disciple, verily I say unto you, he shall in no wise lose his reward.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Though a cup of cold water be thy gift, on which there is nothing laid out, even of this shall a reward be stored up for thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I do all things for the sake of you the receivers.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Seest thou what mighty persuasions He used, and how He opened to them the houses of the whole world?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, He signified that men are their debtors: first, by saying, “The workman is worthy of his hire;” secondly, by sending them forth having nothing; thirdly, by giving them up to wars and fightings in behalf of them that receive them; fourthly, by committing to them miracles also; fifthly, in that He did by their lips introduce peace, the cause of all blessings, into the houses of such as receive them; sixthly, by threatening things more grievous than Sodom to such as receive them not: seventhly, by signifying that as many as welcome them are receiving both Himself and the Father; eighthly, by promising both a prophet’s and a righteous man’s reward: ninthly, by undertaking that the recompenses shall be great, even for a cup of cold water.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Now each one of these things, even by itself, were enough to attract them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For who, tell me, when a leader of armies wounded in innumerable places, and dyed in blood, came in sight, returning after many trophies from war and conflict, would not receive him, throwing open every door in his house?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But who now is like this? one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Therefore He added, “In the name of a disciple, and of a prophet, and of a righteous man;” to instruct thee that not for the worthiness of the visitor, but for the purpose of him that gives welcome, is His reward appointed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For though here He speak of prophets, and righteous men, and disciples, yet elsewhere He bids men receive the veriest outcasts, and punishes such as fail to do so.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For, “Inasmuch as ye did it not to one of the least of these, ye did it not to me;”and the converse again He affirms with respect to the same persons.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since though he may be doing no such great work, he is a man, inhabiting the same world with thee, beholding the same sun having the same soul, the same Lord, a partaker with thee of the same mysteries, called to the same heaven with thee; having a strong claim, his poverty, and his want of necessary food.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now they that waken thee with flutes and pipes in the winter season, and disturb thee without purpose or fruit, depart from thee receiving many gifts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And they that carry about swallows,and smut themselves over,and abuse every one, receive a reward for this their conjuration.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if there come to thee a poor man wanting bread, there is no end of revilings, and reproaches, and charges of idleness, and upbraidings, and insults, and jeers; and thou considerest not with thyself, that thou too art idle, and yet God giveth thee His gifts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me not this, that thou too art doing somewhat, but point me out this rather, if it be anything really needful that thou doest, and art busy about.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if thou tellest one of money-getting, and of traffic, and of the care and increase of thy goods, I also would say unto thee, Not these, but alms, and prayers, and the protection of the injured, and all such things, are truly works, with respect to which we live in thorough idleness.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet God never told us, “Because thou art idle, I light not up the sun for thee; because thou doest nothing of real consequence, I quench the moon, I paralyze the womb of the earth, I restrain the lakes, the fountains, the rivers, I blot out the atmosphere: I withhold the annual rains:” but He gives us all abundantly.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And to some that are not merely idle, but even doing evil, He freely gives the benefit of these things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: When therefore thou seest a poor man, and sayest, “It stops my breath that this fellow, young as he is and healthy, having nothing, would fain be fed in idleness; he is surely some slave and runaway, and hath deserted his proper master:” I bid thee speak these same words to thyself; or rather, permit him freely to speak them unto thee, and he will say with more justice, “It stops my breath that thou, being healthy, art idle, and practisest none of the things which God hath commanded, but having run away from the commandments of thy Lord, goest about dwelling in wickedness, as in a strange land, in drunkenness, in surfeiting, in theft, in extortion, in subverting other men’s houses.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thou indeed imputest idleness, but I evil works; in thy plotting, in thy swearing, in thy lying, in thy spoiling, in thy doing innumerable such things.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this I say, not as making a law in favor of idleness, far from it; but rather very earnestly wishing all to be employed; for sloth is the teacher of all wickedness: but I beseech you not to be unmerciful, nor cruel.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since Paul also, having made infinite complaints, and said, “If any will not work, neither let him eat,” stopped not at this, but added, “But ye, be not weary in well doing.”“Nay, but these things are contradictory.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For if thou hast commanded for them not to eat, how exhortest thou us to give?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I do so, saith He, for I have also commanded to avoid them, and “to have no company with them;” and again I said, “Count them not as enemies, but admonish them;”not making contradictory laws, but such as are quite in unison with each other.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because, if thou art prompt to mercy, both he, the poor man, will soon be rid of his idleness, and thou of thy cruelty.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But he hath many lies and inventions,” you reply.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well, hence again is he pitiable, for that he hath fallen into such distress, as to be hardened even in such doings.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But we, so far from pitying, add even those cruel words, “Hast thou not received once and again?” so we talk.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What then? because he was once fed, hath he no need to be fed again?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou not make these laws for thine own belly also, and say to it likewise, Thou wert filled yesterday, and the day before, seek it not now?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But while thou fillest that beyond measure, even to bursting,from him thou turnest away, when he asks but what is moderate; whereas thou oughtest therefore to pity him, because he is constrained to come to thee every day.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, if nought else incline thee to him, thou shouldest pity him because of this; for by the constraint of his poverty he is forced on these things, and doeth them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And thou dost not pity him, because, being so spoken to, he feels no shame: the reason being, that his want is too strong for him.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, thou instead of pitying, dost even make a show of him; and whereas God hath commanded to give secretly, thou standest exposing publicly him that hath accosted thee, and upbraiding him, for what ought to move thy pity.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if thou art not minded to give, to what end add reproach, and bruise that weary and wretched soul?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: He came as into a harbor, seeking help at thine hands; why stir up waves, and make the storm more grievous?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why dost thou condemn him of meanness?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What? had he thought to hear such things, would he have come to thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Or if he actually came foreseeing this, good cause therefore both to pity him, and to shudder at thine own cruelty, that not even so, when thou seest an inexorable necessity laid upon him, dost thou become more gentle, nor judgest him to have a sufficient excuse for his importunity in the dread of hunger, but accusest him of impudence: and yet hast thou often thyself practised greater impudence, yea in respect of grievous matters.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while here the very impudence brings with it ground of pardon, we, often doing things punishable, brazen it out: and when we ought to bear all that in mind, and be humble, we even trample on those miserable men, and when they ask medicines, we add to their wounds.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I say, if thou wilt not give, yet why dost thou strike?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If thou wilt not be bounteous, yet why be insolent?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But he submits not to be put off in any other way.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Well then, as that wise man commanded,so do.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “Answer him peaceable words with meekness.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For not of his own accord, surely, is he so very importunate.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For there is not, there cannot be, any man desiring to be put to shame for its own sake.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: How much soever any may contend, I cannot yield ever to be convinced that a man who was living in plenty would choose to beg.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let no man then beguile us with arguments.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But although Paul saith, “If any will not work, neither let him eat,”to them he saith it; but to us he saith not this, but, on the contrary, “Be not weary in well doing.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Even thus do we at home; when any two are striving with each other, we take each apart, and give them the opposite advice.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This did God also, and Moses.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For while to God he said, “If thou wilt forgive them their sin, forgive it; else blot me out also;”them on the contrary he commanded to slay one another, and all that pertained to them.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yet these things are contrary; nevertheless, both looked to one end.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Again, God said to Moses in the hearing of the Jews, “Let me alone, that I may consume the people,”(for though they were not present when God was saying this, yet they were to hear it afterwards): but privately He gives him directions of the opposite tenor.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And this, Moses upon constraint revealed afterwards, thus saying, “What? did I conceive them, that thou sayest to me, Carry them, as a nurse would carry the sucking child in her bosom?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: These things are done also in houses, and often a father while he blames the tutor in private for having used his child reproachfully, saying, “Be not rough, nor hard,” to the youth speaks in the contrary way, “Though thou be reproached unjustly, bear it;” out of those opposites making up some one wholesome result.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Thus also Paul said to such as are in health and beg, “If any man will not work, neither let him eat,” that he may urge them into employment: but to such as can show mercy, “Ye, for your part, be not weary in well doing:” that he may lead them to give alms.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: So also, when he was admonishing those of the Gentiles, in his Epistle to the Romans, not to be highminded against the Jews, he brought forward also the wild olive, and he seems to be saying one thing to these, another to those.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not therefore fall away into cruelty, but let us listen to Paul, saying, “Be not weary in well doing;” let us listen to the Lord, who saith, “Give to every man that asketh of thee,”and, “Be ye merciful as your Father.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And though He hath spoken of many things, He hath nowhere used this expression, but with regard to our deeds of mercy only.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For nothing so equals us with God, as doing good.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But nothing is more shameless,” saith one, “than a poor man.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, I pray thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Because he runs up, and cries out after thee?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Wilt thou then let me point out, how we are more importunate than they, and very shameless?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Remember, I say, now at the season of the fast, how often, when thy table was spread at eventide, and thou hadst called thy ministering servant; on his moving rather leisurely,thou hast overset everything, kicking, insulting, reviling, merely about a little delay; although fully assured, that if not immediately, yet a little after thou shalt enjoy thy victuals.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Upon which thou dost not call thyself impudent, changed as thou art into a wild beast for nothing; but the poor man, alarmed and trembling about his greater interests (for not about delay, but about famine, is all his fear), him dost thou call audacious, and shameless, and impudent, and all the most opprobrious names?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, how is this anything but extreme impudence.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But these things we do not consider: therefore we account such men troublesome: since if we at all searched into our own doings, and compared them with theirs, we should not have thought them intolerable.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Be not then a severe judge.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if thou wert clear of all sins, not even then would the law of God permit thee to be strict in searching out other men’s sins.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if the Pharisee perished on this account, what defense are we to find?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: If He suffer not such as have done well to be bitter in searching out other men’s doings, much less them that have offended.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Let us not then be savage, nor cruel, not without natural feeling, not implacable, not worse than wild beasts.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For I know many to have gone even so far in brutishness, as for a little trouble to slight famishing persons, and to say these words: “I have no servant now with me; we are far from home; there is no money-changer that I know.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Didst thou promise the greater, and dost thou not fulfill the less?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: To save thy walking a little way, doth he perish with hunger?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Oh insolence!

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why, if it were ten furlongs to be walked, oughtest thou to be backward?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Doth it not even come into thy mind that so thy reward is made greater?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For whereas, when thou givest, thou receivest reward for the gift only: when thou thyself also goest, for this again is appointed thee a recompense.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Yea, the patriarch himself we admire for this, that in his own person he ran to the herd, and snatched up the calf,and that, when he had three hundred and eighteen servants born in his house.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But now some are filled with so much pride, as to do these things by servants, and not to be ashamed.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “But dost thou require me to do these things myself?” one may say.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “How then shall I not seem to be vainglorious?”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Nay, but as it is, thou art led by another kind of vainglory to do this, being ashamed to be seen talking with a poor man.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But I am in no respect strict about this; only give, whether by thyself or by another thou art minded to do so; and do not accuse, do not smite, do not revile.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For medicines, not wounds, doth he need who comes unto thee; mercy, not a sword.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: For tell me, if any one who had been smitten with a stone, and had received a wound in his head, were to let go all others, and run unto thy knees, drenched in his blood; wouldest thou indeed smite him with another stone, and add unto him another wound?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: I, for my part, think not; but even as it was, thou wouldest endeavor to cure it.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Why then doest thou the contrary with respect to the poor?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Knowest thou not how much power a word hath, both to raise up, and to cast down?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: “For a word,” it is said, “is better than a gift.”

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Dost thou not consider that thou art thrusting the sword into thyself, and art receiving a more grievous wound, when he, being reviled, silently withdraws, with groans and many tears?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Since indeed of God he is sent unto thee.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Consider then, in insulting him, upon whom thou art causing the insult to pass; when God indeed sends him unto thee, and commands thee to give, but thou, so far from giving, dost even insult him on his coming.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: And if thou art not aware how exceedingly amiss this is, look at it as among men, and then thou wilt fully know the greatness of the sin.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: As thus: if a servant of thine had been commanded by thee to go to another servant, who had money of thine, to receive it, and were to come back not only with empty hands, but also with despiteful usage; what wouldest thou not do to him that had wrought the insult?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: What penalty wouldest thou not exact, as though, after this, it were thyself that had been ill used?

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: This reckoning do thou make in regard of God also; for truly it is He that sends the poor to us, and of His we give, if indeed we do give.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: But if, besides not giving, we also send them away insulted, consider how many bolts, how many thunders, that which we are doing deserves.

verses.book: The Homilies of St. John Chrysostom.
verses.text: Duly considering then all these things, let us both bridle our tongue, and put away inhumanity, and let us stretch forth the hand to give alms, and not with money only, but with words also, let us relieve such as are in need; that we may both escape the punishment for reviling, and may inherit the kingdom which is for blessing and almsgiving, by the grace and love towards man of our Lord Jesus Christ, to whom be glory and might forever and ever.
